g.text: The TPS61086 device is a high-frequency, high-efficiency DC-to-DC converter with an integrated 2.0-A, 0.13-Ω power switch capable of providing an output voltage up

g.desc: The TPS61086 device is a high-frequency, high-efficiency DC-to-DC converter with an integrated 2.0-A, 0.13-Ω power switch capable of providing an output voltage up

g.text: to 18.5 V. The implemented boost converter is based on a fixed frequency of 1.2-MHz, pulse-width-modulation (PWM) controller that allows the use of small external

g.desc: to 18.5 V. The implemented boost converter is based on a fixed frequency of 1.2-MHz, pulse-width-modulation (PWM) controller that allows the use of small external

g.text: inductors and capacitors and provides fast transient response.

g.desc: inductors and capacitors and provides fast transient response.

g.text: At light-load, the device can operate in Power Save Mode with pulse-frequency-modulation (PFM) to improve the efficiency while keeping a low-output voltage ripple.

g.desc: At light-load, the device can operate in Power Save Mode with pulse-frequency-modulation (PFM) to improve the efficiency while keeping a low-output voltage ripple.

g.text: For very noise-sensitive applications, the device can be forced to PWM Mode operation over the entire load range by pulling the MODE pin high. The external

g.desc: For very noise-sensitive applications, the device can be forced to PWM Mode operation over the entire load range by pulling the MODE pin high. The external

g.text: compensation allows optimizing the application for specific conditions. A capacitor connected to the soft-start pin minimizes inrush current at start-up.

g.desc: compensation allows optimizing the application for specific conditions. A capacitor connected to the soft-start pin minimizes inrush current at start-up.

g.style: fill:none; stroke:none;

g.text: COMP (pin 1): error amplifier compensation pin; connect the Rcomp + Ccomp network to AGND

g.desc: COMP (pin 1): error amplifier compensation pin; connect the Rcomp + Ccomp network to AGND

g.text: to stabilize the loop (do not route it near SW).

g.desc: to stabilize the loop (do not route it near SW).

g.text: FB (pin 2): feedback input; connect it to the R1/R2 resistor divider from VOUT (after the diode)

g.desc: FB (pin 2): feedback input; connect it to the R1/R2 resistor divider from VOUT (after the diode)

g.text: to set the output voltage (e.g. 6 V).

g.desc: to set the output voltage (e.g. 6 V).

g.text: EN (pin 3): enable / shutdown; high level = active, low level = off. Often tied to IN for "always

g.desc: EN (pin 3): enable / shutdown; high level = active, low level = off. Often tied to IN for "always

g.text: ON", or driven by a GPIO.

g.desc: ON", or driven by a GPIO.

g.text: AGND (pin 4): analog ground (reference for sensitive signals: FB/COMP/SS/MODE). Keep it

g.desc: AGND (pin 4): analog ground (reference for sensitive signals: FB/COMP/SS/MODE). Keep it

g.text: "clean" and connect it to the GND plane in a controlled way.

g.desc: "clean" and connect it to the GND plane in a controlled way.

g.text: PGND (pin 5): power ground (return path for switching currents). Connect it to the GND plane

g.desc: PGND (pin 5): power ground (return path for switching currents). Connect it to the GND plane

g.text: with a short/wide copper area close to the power capacitors.

g.desc: with a short/wide copper area close to the power capacitors.

g.text: SW (pins 6-7): switch node; connect it to L and the Schottky diode. This is the noisiest node →

g.desc: SW (pins 6-7): switch node; connect it to L and the Schottky diode. This is the noisiest node →

g.text: keep the trace short, minimize the loop area, and keep it away from FB/COMP.

g.desc: keep the trace short, minimize the loop area, and keep it away from FB/COMP.

g.text: IN (pin 8): power input (2.3-6 V); decouple it as close as possible with Cby ~1 µF + Cin (e.g. 10

g.desc: IN (pin 8): power input (2.3-6 V); decouple it as close as possible with Cby ~1 µF + Cin (e.g. 10

g.text: MODE (pin 9): mode selection; MODE = High → Forced PWM (more stable frequency, better for

g.desc: MODE (pin 9): mode selection; MODE = High → Forced PWM (more stable frequency, better for

g.text: noise), MODE = Low → PFM (better efficiency at light load).

g.desc: noise), MODE = Low → PFM (better efficiency at light load).

g.text: SS (pin 10): soft-start; add a capacitor (Css) to limit inrush current at startup. Left open = no

g.desc: SS (pin 10): soft-start; add a capacitor (Css) to limit inrush current at startup. Left open = no
